module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Project: Build the Field Notes Repository
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: A neighborhood garden has useful notes but no history. Carry the same small project from an ordinary folder to one clean, trustworthy first snapshot.

sketch0.title: The Garden Notes Need a Beginning

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Initialize the Field Notes Repository
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt:
The garden notes are still an ordinary folder. Enter `garden-notes/`, initialize it with `main` as the first branch, and print the current branch name.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: Enter the requested project directory before running Git.

help.concept: Initialization creates repository metadata inside the current folder; it does not create a commit.
help.hint_1: Enter the requested project directory before running Git.
help.hint_2: Use the requested `main` initial branch and verify the repository afterward.

try_git_repo_inspection.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.
try_git_repo_inspection.solutionCode:
cd garden-notes
git init -b main
git branch --show-current
try_git_repo_inspection.instructions:
The garden notes are still an ordinary folder. Enter `garden-notes/`, initialize it with `main` as the first branch, and print the current branch name.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

main_sh.content:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf garden-notes
mkdir -p garden-notes/observations
printf '# Garden Field Notes\n' > garden-notes/README.md
printf 'Plot A: soil is damp after rain.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-a.txt
printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt

main_sh.content:
cd garden-notes
git init -b main
git branch --show-current

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:

printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt

try_git_config.title: Set the Repository Identity
try_git_config.prompt:
The repository now exists. Set `user.name` to `ZoeSkoul Learner` and `user.email` to `learner@zoeskoul.local` for this repository only, then print both local values.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_git_config.hint: Run the commands inside the initialized repository.

help.concept: Repository-local configuration gives commits an author without changing machine-wide Git settings.
help.hint_1: Run the commands inside the initialized repository.
help.hint_2: Set both `user.name` and `user.email`, then read them back if you need to verify.

try_git_config.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.
try_git_config.solutionCode:
cd garden-notes
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git config --local --get user.name
git config --local --get user.email
try_git_config.instructions:
The repository now exists. Set `user.name` to `ZoeSkoul Learner` and `user.email` to `learner@zoeskoul.local` for this repository only, then print both local values.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

main_sh.content:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf garden-notes
mkdir -p garden-notes/observations

printf 'Plot A: soil is damp after rain.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-a.txt
printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt
git -C garden-notes init -q -b main

main_sh.content:
cd garden-notes
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git config --local --get user.name
git config --local --get user.email

try_git_init.title: Read the Untracked Project Files
try_git_init.prompt:
The identity is ready, but Git is not tracking the notes yet. Use short status and identify the README plus both observation files as untracked.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_git_init.hint: Use the exact paths, branch, and message named in the task.

help.concept: Repository state is the source of truth: read it, make one deliberate change, and verify it again.
help.hint_1: Use the exact paths, branch, and message named in the task.
help.hint_2: Run `git status --short` after the change and compare the result with the requested final state.

try_git_init.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.

try_git_stage_files.title: Stage the Field Notes
try_git_stage_files.prompt:
Prepare the first snapshot by staging `README.md` and everything under `observations/`. Then list the staged file names so you can verify the boundary before committing.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_git_stage_files.hint: Inspect the staged diff before committing.

help.concept: A commit records only the staged snapshot and gives it a message another reader can understand.
help.hint_1: Inspect the staged diff before committing.
help.hint_2: Use the exact requested message, then verify the newest history entry and status.

try_git_stage_files.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.
try_git_stage_files.solutionCode:
cd garden-notes
git add README.md observations/
git diff --staged --name-only
try_git_stage_files.instructions:
Prepare the first snapshot by staging `README.md` and everything under `observations/`. Then list the staged file names so you can verify the boundary before committing.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

try_git_commit.title: Create the First Snapshot
try_git_commit.prompt:
The three project files are staged. Commit them with the message `Start garden field notes`, read the one-line history, and finish by checking that the working tree is clean.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_git_commit.hint: Start with the compact history to find the commit you need.

help.concept: Log gives the timeline overview; show opens the contents of one recorded snapshot.
help.hint_1: Start with the compact history to find the commit you need.
help.hint_2: Check the order and message rather than relying on a machine-specific hash.

try_git_commit.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this milestone.
# The project opens at the state reached in the previous step.
try_git_commit.solutionCode:
cd garden-notes
git commit -m "Start garden field notes"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_git_commit.instructions:
The three project files are staged. Commit them with the message `Start garden field notes`, read the one-line history, and finish by checking that the working tree is clean.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

# Use the terminal for this milestone.

moduleProject.title: Build the Field Notes Repository
moduleProject.description:
The garden team has notes worth keeping, but no dependable record of how the project began. You will create that beginning yourself: initialize the folder, set a local identity, read what Git sees, choose the first snapshot, and commit it.

Each milestone opens with the previous one already complete, so the sequence feels cumulative without exposing platform setup. Finish with one commit named `Start garden field notes` and a clean working tree.

intro-to-git-repository.title: A Field Notebook Becomes a Repository
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown:
The garden team has a small folder of field notes but no reliable history. Your job is to turn that ordinary folder into the team’s first Git repository.

The project follows the same path as the module: initialize on `main`, set a repository-only identity, read the untracked state, stage the intended notes, and create one clear initial commit. Each step restores the completed state from the previous step behind the scenes, so you can focus only on the next Git decision.

Finish with one commit named `Start garden field notes` and a clean working tree.
